methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $141M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 4 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Biotech & Health. Range: $539M - $10.3B.

data.narrative:
AAVantgarde Bio is a Series B-stage biotech company founded by Alberto Auricchio and Natalia Misciattelli that has raised $141M to date. The valuation analysis draws from a comparable universe of 4 private peers in Biotech & Health at the same or adjacent stage, plus 3 public-market reference points (RXRX, TEM, ONT.L).

Two valuation methods apply quantitatively. The Stage-Progression method uses the $141M total raised as a proxy base value and applies a 1.9x multiplier typical of Series B-to-next-stage advancement, yielding $268M. The Peer-Median method anchors to a cohort of 4 same-sector, same/adjacent-stage companies: Colossal Biosciences ($10.3B), Chai Discovery ($3.8B), New Limit ($3.1B), and Eikon Therapeutics ($539M). The median of this peer set is $3.5B, with a range of $539M to $10.3B. Public-comp anchoring via P/S multiples (6.9x median across the 3 public tickers) is not applicable due to absence of disclosed revenue for the target.

The triangulated analysis across two applicable methods supports a valuation range of $228M (low) to $4.0B (high), with a median of $1.9B. The wide dispersion reflects material uncertainty around stage-specific risk premia and peer comparability; the peer-median approach may overweight later-stage outliers (Colossal at $10.3B), while stage-progression relies on the representativeness of the 1.9x multiplier for this company's risk profile and market conditions.
